lever: Guard the exact scalar-I64 sub-jaxpr while family cond carry > literal and body carry - positive literal, then compute each lane's termination count closed-form instead of simulating the active-mask loop. Overflow-risk and unsupported cases fall back to the existing active-mask implementation.

isomorphism.ordering: Output lanes are written in the same batch order as the prior active-mask loop.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: No sorting, tie-breaking, or branch-choice ordering is present.
isomorphism.floating_point: Accepted path is I64-only; no floating-point operations are introduced.
isomorphism.rng: No RNG or effects are present in the accepted sub-jaxpr shape.
isomorphism.arithmetic: For accepted positive-step monotone lanes, final carry equals the same repeated wrapping_sub sequence before any wrap can occur; overflow-risk cases decline to the existing loop. The max_iter boundary preserves the existing behavior where condition true on the final allowed iteration is still an error.
isomorphism.fallback: Non-I64 literals, non-Gt conditions, non-Sub bodies, nonpositive steps, overflow-risk arithmetic, malformed shapes, and all other while sub-jaxprs keep the old active-mask path.
